vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.2d6_plus_skill: Nearly every task is a 'throw' of 2d6 against a target number, most often 8+, with dice modifiers from skills and characteristics; Book 1 lists throws like 'Admin 7+' per situation.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.lifepath: Characters enlist in one of six services (Navy, Marines, Army, Scouts, Merchants, Other) and resolve four-year terms of enlistment, survival, commission, promotion, and reenlistment throws before play begins.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.lifepath_generation: Character generation is itself a push-your-luck minigame: each extra term adds skills and mustering-out benefits but risks failing the survival throw, which in the original rules kills the character before play starts.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.aging: Starting at age 34, each additional four-year term forces aging saves against Strength, Dexterity, and Endurance, with characteristic loss on failure; anagathic drugs can stave off aging at great cost and risk.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: Death is codified twice: a failed survival throw during prior-service generation kills the character, and in combat a character whose Strength, Dexterity, and Endurance are all reduced to zero dies.
vector_explanations.character.progression.careers_system: Each service has its own enlistment, survival, commission, promotion, and skill tables, plus rank titles and mustering-out benefits (cash, weapons, TAS membership, even a free Scout ship or ship shares).
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: The six characteristics — Strength, Dexterity, Endurance, Intelligence, Education, Social Standing — are each rolled on 2d6 in order and recorded as the hexadecimal UPP string (e.g., 777A98).
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.space_jump_travel: Interstellar travel uses jump drives rated Jump-1 through Jump-6 parsecs; every jump takes about one week in jumpspace regardless of distance, and misjumps threaten ships jumping within 100 diameters of a world.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.astrogation_navigation: Plotting a jump requires the navigator and the ship's computer running generate/jump programs; jumping without proper data or inside a gravity well risks a misjump that can fling the ship parsecs off course or destroy it.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.fuel_tracking: Jump fuel is a hard formula (0.1 × tonnage × jump number) plus power plant fuel; unrefined fuel skimmed from gas giants or oceans risks misjump, making refueling stops a core loop of any voyage.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.cargo_capacity: Ships are measured in 100-ton-plus displacement hulls with cargo holds rated in tons; every speculative lot and freight consignment occupies hold tonnage, constraining what a free trader can lift.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Book 2 stats every starship with hull tonnage, jump and maneuver drive letters, power plant rating, computer model, hardpoints, and monthly overhead — a full vehicle sheet distinct from any crew member.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Starships require crew by role and tonnage — pilot, navigator, engineers (one per 35 tons of drives), medic, steward, and gunners — each with salary costs and skill requirements.
vector_explanations.vehicles.combat.component_damage: Book 2 ship combat resolves hits on a damage table that knocks out specific systems — maneuver drive, jump drive, power plant, computer, turrets — degrading the ship before destroying it.
vector_explanations.economy.finance.loan_interest: The iconic ship mortgage: a free trader is financed over 40 years at monthly payments of roughly 1/240th of the ship's price, and meeting the bank note drives the entire campaign economy.
vector_explanations.economy.trading.cargo_shipping: Standard rates are codified — Cr1,000 per ton of freight, high/middle/low passage tickets at fixed prices, with low berths requiring a survival throw on revival — making shipping a bookkept revenue stream.
vector_explanations.economy.market.commodity_speculation: Speculative trade rolls on cargo tables with purchase and resale dice modifiers from world trade classifications, letting merchants buy low on agricultural worlds and gamble on selling high elsewhere.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.tech_levels: Every world has a rolled tech level that gates what equipment can be bought or repaired there, from TL primitive to starfaring, and weapon/armor availability is keyed to it.
vector_explanations.simulation.astronomy.sector_generation: Subsector generation procedures roll world presence per hex, then starport class, size, atmosphere, hydrographics, population, government, law level, and tech level into a Universal World Profile.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.availability_r: Equipment access is governed by world law level (which bans weapon classes above thresholds) and tech level, with throws to avoid harassment by authorities when carrying restricted arms.
vector_explanations.exploration.procedures.reaction_rolls: Encounters with NPCs and animals use a 2d6 reaction table ranging from violent attack to genuinely friendly, modified by circumstances, so first contact outcomes are procedurally generated.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Personal combat resolves fire across defined range bands (close, short, medium, long, very long) with per-weapon DM matrices, so a shotgun and a laser carbine perform very differently by band.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.strength_based: Carried weight is limited by Strength: characters bear up to STR kilograms unencumbered, roughly double with penalties, so gear loads directly trade off against combat effectiveness.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.morale_checks: Groups of NPCs and military units test morale on 2d6 when taking casualties or when leaders fall, breaking and routing on failure, per the Book 1 combat rules.

title: Traveller (Mongoose 2nd Edition)
resolution_core: 2d6 + skill + characteristic DM vs difficulty 8+ (Effect = margin)

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.2d6_plus_skill: Checks roll 2d6 plus skill level plus a characteristic DM against Average difficulty 8+, with the task chain and timeframe rules layered on the same throw.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.degrees_of_success: Effect equals the roll total minus 8: Effect 6+ is exceptional success, Effect -6 or worse exceptional failure, and Effect feeds directly into damage and task chains.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.advantage_disadvant: Boons and Banes have the check roll 3d6 and keep the best or worst two dice respectively, replacing stacks of situational modifiers with a single dice manipulation.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.tiered_difficu: Task difficulties run a named ladder — Simple (2+), Easy (4+), Routine (6+), Average (8+), Difficult (10+), Very Difficult (12+), Formidable (14+) — set by the referee before the throw.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Task chains link sequential checks so the Effect of one roll grants a DM from -3 to +2 to the next, letting a navigator's plot quality flow into the engineer's jump throw.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.lifepath: Careers are resolved in four-year terms with qualification, survival, event, advancement, and mishap tables; failing survival throws you onto the mishap table and out of the career rather than killing you outright.
vector_explanations.character.progression.careers_system: A dozen careers with three assignments each provide rank tables, skill tables, and mustering-out benefits, plus the connections rule granting bonus skills for shared history between player characters.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.aging: From age 34, each term ends with an aging roll of 2d6 minus terms served; negative totals strip physical characteristic points, and anagathics defer the crisis at Cr2500+ per month with withdrawal risks.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Six characteristics are rolled on 2d6 and expressed as the classic hexadecimal Universal Personality Profile, each granting a DM from -3 to +3 on checks.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_defaults: Attempting a skill you lack entirely imposes DM-3, while background skills and basic training grant a suite of level-0 skills that let travellers attempt tasks without penalty.
vector_explanations.character.skills.specialty_trees: Skills at level 1+ split into specialities — Engineer (J-drive), Gun Combat (slug), Pilot (spacecraft) — so training deepens along branches rather than one flat number.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.space_jump_travel: Jump drives rated Jump-1 to Jump-6 cross that many parsecs in roughly 148 hours; jumping within 100 diameters of a massive body invites a misjump roll on the jump table.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.astrogation_navigation: Plotting a course is an Astrogation check whose Effect chains into the engineer's jump initiation, with bad plots increasing misjump chances and travel time deviation.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.fuel_tracking: Ships track refined versus unrefined fuel tonnage; jumps consume 10% of hull tonnage per jump number, and skimming gas giants with unrefined fuel risks drive complications.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Ship combat assigns crew to stations — pilot, gunners, engineer, sensor operator, captain — each with defined actions per round like Evasive Action, Jamming, or overloading the drives.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Every ship has a stat block of hull points, armour, thrust rating, jump rating, power, computer bandwidth, and hardpoints, maintained separately from any crew member's sheet.
vector_explanations.vehicles.combat.component_damage: Critical hits in space combat strike specific locations — sensors, power plant, fuel, weapons, armour, hull, bridge, crew — with severity levels 1-6 that progressively cripple systems.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.tech_levels: Equipment lists are keyed to Tech Levels 0-15+; a world's TL caps what can be bought or serviced there, and higher-TL gear commands premium prices on lower-TL worlds.
vector_explanations.simulation.astronomy.sector_generation: World generation rolls the full Universal World Profile — starport, size, atmosphere, hydrographics, population, government, law, tech — and derives trade codes that drive the economy.
vector_explanations.economy.market.commodity_speculation: Speculative trade cross-references trade goods against world trade codes for purchase and sale DMs on 3d6 price tables, rewarding brokers who match cargo to the right market.
vector_explanations.economy.trading.cargo_shipping: Freight lots pay by the ton on published tables, and passengers come in high, middle, and basic passage classes found via Broker or Streetwise checks, forming the free trader's baseline income.
vector_explanations.economy.finance.loan_interest: Standard ship financing is a 40-year mortgage with monthly payments around 1/240th of the purchase price, so the party's far trader arrives saddled with crushing debt.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.lifestyle_cos: Monthly living costs scale with Social Standing on a published table from subsistence to high living, quietly draining the crew's credits between jobs.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each combat round a traveller takes one Significant action and one Minor action (or three Minors), with free actions like talking layered on top.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is 2d6 plus DEX (or INT) DM rolled once at combat start, with tactics checks and hasty actions shifting the count up or down.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Weapons list ranges in metres; attacks at short range gain DM+1 while long and extended range impose DM-2 and DM-4, and pistols simply cannot reach rifle distances effectively.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.armor_as_soak: Armour subtracts its protection rating from every incoming damage roll before dice hit characteristics, with damage then depleting END, STR, and DEX in order.

title: Stars Without Number: Revised Edition
resolution_core: 2d6 + skill + attribute mod vs difficulty 6-12 (checks); d20 + attack bonus vs AC (combat)

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.2d6_plus_skill: Skill checks roll 2d6 plus skill level plus attribute modifier against difficulties of 6 (routine), 8 (hard), or 10+ (extreme), with unskilled attempts taking a -1 penalty.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Attacks are old-school: d20 plus attack bonus plus skill and attribute versus Armor Class, keeping combat compatible with OSR modules while checks stay 2d6.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Characters pick Warrior, Expert, or Psychic — or the Adventurer option combining two partial classes — each with distinct abilities like the Warrior's once-per-scene guaranteed hit or the Expert's skill reroll.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Backgrounds like Barbarian, Clergy, or Spacer grant a free skill plus either a fixed package or rolls on growth and learning tables, tying competence to pre-adventuring life.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.rolled_stats: Attributes are 3d6 in order (with the option to set one to 14 afterward) or a standard array, with modifiers only ranging from -2 to +2 to keep skill levels dominant.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Skills run level-0 to level-4, bought with skill points on a rising cost schedule, and level-4 mastery is explicitly capped until high character levels.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Characters advance through levels 1-10 on a fixed XP table, with hit points rerolled from the full pool each level, keeping the old-school lethality curve.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Physical, Evasion, and Mental saving throws each equal 15 minus character level minus relevant attribute modifiers, succeeded on a d20 roll equal or over.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.discipline_trees: Psychics buy into six disciplines — Biopsionics, Metapsionics, Precognition, Telekinesis, Telepathy, Teleportation — each with a core technique that scales by level and picks from a technique tree.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.power_points: Powers are fueled by a per-day Effort pool; techniques commit Effort for the scene, the day, or indefinitely, so a psychic's real resource is how much Effort stays uncommitted.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.psychic_strain: Torching lets a desperate psychic overload for extra Effort at the cost of permanent attribute damage and potential feral madness, making unmastered power genuinely dangerous.
vector_explanations.simulation.faction.faction_turns: Factions with Force, Cunning, and Wealth ratings take a monthly faction turn, spending FacCreds on actions like Expand Influence or Attack, with assets battling on their own attack/defense stats.
vector_explanations.simulation.astronomy.sector_generation: The GM tools generate an eight-by-ten hex sector, rolling system placement, world attributes, and two evocative world tags per planet (e.g., Regressed, Police State) that seed adventures.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.tech_levels: Worlds and gear are rated TL0-TL5; pretech (TL5) artifacts of the Mandate era are scarce treasures, and a world's TL bounds what its markets and shipyards can support.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.starship_modificat: Ships install fittings — spike drives, fuel scoops, drop pods, weapons — within hull class limits for power and mass, and shipyard work lets crews retrofit their vessel over a campaign.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cybernetics: Cyberware like reflex boosters and body arsenals imposes System Strain that competes with the same track used by stims and biotech healing, capping how much chrome a body tolerates.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.space_jump_travel: Spike drives drill through metadimensional space at drive rating hexes per journey leg, taking about six days per hex, with drive-rating upgrades expanding reachable space.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.astrogation_navigation: Trying to trim course time or drill beyond rated range calls for Pilot/Navigation checks where failure can maroon the ship in deep space or worse, per the spike drill mishap rules.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Ship combat gives each department — Captain, Gunnery, Engineering, Piloting, Comms — its own actions funded by the captain's Command Points generated each round.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Ships have hull class (fighter to capital), HP, AC, armor, speed, and free mass/power/hardpoint budgets, statted independently of their crews.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.fuel_tracking: A ship carries fuel for one spike drill by default; extended range needs fuel bunkers or scoops, and running dry in an empty hex is an explicit, lethal failure state.
vector_explanations.campaign.management.adventure_hooks_tabl: The GM section supplies one-roll tables for adventure seeds, NPCs, urban encounters, and ruins, designed so a sandbox referee can improvise a session from a handful of dice.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Hit points are low and lethal — dropping to 0 HP means death in six rounds unless stabilized with a Heal check, and stabilized characters wake wounded, not healed.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each round grants a Main Action, a Move action, and any number of On Turn actions like dropping prone, with actions such as Total Defense or Screen Ally consuming the Main Action.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Combatants roll 1d8 plus Dexterity modifier for initiative, acting in descending order, with sides able to group-roll for speed at the referee's option.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.starting_packa: New characters take a class-and-background-keyed equipment package (or roll credits and shop), so a Spacer Warrior starts kitted for shipboard violence without a shopping session.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.slot_based_invento: Encumbrance counts readied items up to half Strength and stowed items up to full Strength, with movement penalties past those slots, replacing weight bookkeeping.

title: GURPS 4th Edition
resolution_core: 3d6 roll-under skill or attribute; margin of success governs outcomes

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_attr: Everything resolves as 3d6 rolled at or under an effective skill or attribute; attacks roll under weapon skill, defenses under Dodge, Parry, or Block, all on the same bell curve.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Quick Contests compare margins of two simultaneous roll-under attempts (e.g., Stealth vs. Perception), while Regular Contests iterate rolls until one side alone succeeds.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.margin_of_success: Margin of success and failure is read constantly — it decides Quick Contests, sets how long a task takes, and margins of 10+ convert ordinary failure into disaster.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: A roll of 3 or 4 always crits (as does 5-6 at high skill), and a critical hit in combat denies the target any active defense and rolls on the critical hit table.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: Rolls of 18 (or 17 at low skill, or margin 10+) are critical failures, sending attacks to the critical miss table where weapons are dropped, broken, or strike allies.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.reroll_resources: The Luck advantage (15 points) lets a player roll three times and keep the best result once per half hour of play, with Extraordinary Luck cutting the recharge further.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: ST, DX, IQ, and HT start at 10 and are bought up or sold down per level — 10 points per level of ST or HT, 20 per level of DX or IQ — from the campaign's point budget.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.derived_attributes: Secondary characteristics — HP from ST, Will and Perception from IQ, FP from HT, Basic Speed from (HT+DX)/4, Basic Move — are derived but individually adjustable with points.
vector_explanations.character.progression.build_points: Characters are built and advanced with one currency: character points buy attributes, advantages, and skills at creation, and earned points (typically 1-5 per session) buy more of the same in play.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Perks are 1-point mini-advantages like Honest Face or a signature weapon quirk, giving fine-grained texture below the cost of full advantages.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Disadvantages from One Eye to Code of Honor refund points up to a campaign disadvantage limit, and many come with self-control rolls (6, 9, 12, or 15) that price how often they bite.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_defaults: Unstudied skills default to an attribute or related skill at fixed penalties (e.g., Guns defaults to DX-4), and point costs are discounted when buying up from a default.
vector_explanations.character.skills.attribute_skill_pairing: Every skill hangs off an attribute at a difficulty grade — Easy, Average, Hard, Very Hard — which sets the cost ladder for relative skill levels like DX+2 or IQ-1.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.weapon_properties_tags: Weapon tables carry damage type (cutting, impaling, crushing multipliers), reach, parry modifier, minimum ST, accuracy, rate of fire, and bulk, all mechanically live in combat.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.armor_types: Armor grants Damage Resistance by location and construction, from leather to advanced composites, with DR subtracting from damage before wounding multipliers apply.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.hit_locations: The hit location table assigns penalties and effects — skull at -7 with a x4 wounding multiplier, vitals x3 for impaling, limbs crippled at half HP — making anatomy tactically real.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.called_shots: Attackers may target specific locations at listed penalties to bypass armor gaps or force crippling wounds, the classic -7 eye shot being the extreme case.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Grappling uses DX or Wrestling to grab, then Quick Contests of ST for takedowns, pins, chokes, and arm locks, each with defined effects on the victim's actions.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.hex_grid: Tactical combat runs on a one-yard hex map with Move costs per hex, explicit rules for occupying, entering, and being adjacent, per the Basic Set's tactical combat chapter.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.facing: Each figure has facing defining front, side, and back hexes; attacks from the rear deny active defenses, and turning more than one hex-side costs movement.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Ranged fire uses the Size and Speed/Range Table, a logarithmic penalty ladder where each near-doubling of distance adds -1, combined with target size and speed.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Turn order follows Basic Speed (derived from DX and HT), highest first, with no initiative roll — a faster body simply always acts sooner in the second-by-second turn cycle.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: HP equal ST by default; at 0 HP and below you roll HT each turn to stay conscious, and injury is tracked in raw points modified by wounding multipliers.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.massive_damage: Any single wound over half HP is a major wound forcing an HT roll against knockdown and stunning, with hit-location modifiers making head wounds far worse.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.lingering_injuries: Limbs and extremities are crippled by wounds exceeding thresholds (HP/2 for a hand), with rolls after the fight deciding whether the crippling is lasting or permanent.
vector_explanations.combat.status.stun_daze: Physical and mental stunning impose -4 to defenses and lost actions until an IQ or HT roll recovers, and knockdown sends fighters prone with dropped weapons.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At -1xHP you roll HT or die, repeating at each further multiple of HP down to automatic death at -5xHP, with mortal wounds lingering in between.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.skill_roll_casting: In the default magic system each spell is its own IQ/Hard skill rolled at 3d6, with distance penalties, and skill 15+ reduces energy costs and casting times.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.mana_points: Spells burn Fatigue Points (or Powerstone energy) as listed per spell, with maintenance costs per period, so a wizard's endurance is a literal mana pool.
vector_explanations.magic.structure.schools_of_magic: Spells are organized into colleges (Fire, Healing, Necromantic, etc.) with prerequisite chains inside each college gating access to the powerful capstones.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.fear_tests: Fright Checks roll against Will; failure margin adds to a 3d6 roll on the Fright Check Table, producing outcomes from stunning to phobias, faints, and acquired quirks.
vector_explanations.logistics.encumbrance.weight_based: Basic Lift (ST x ST / 5 pounds) defines five encumbrance levels from None to Extra-Heavy, each stepping down Move and Dodge — carried weight is exactly accounted.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.exhaustion: Fatigue Points are spent by exertion, heat, starvation, and spells; below 1/3 FP halves Move and Dodge, and hitting 0 FP risks collapse, all on a tracked pool.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.tech_levels: TL0-TL12 rates settings and gear; characters have a personal TL, equipment lists are TL-keyed, and Low TL/High TL traits price being ahead of or behind the world.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_levels: The Wealth trait from Dead Broke to Multimillionaire multiplies starting funds, while Status levels and monthly cost of living tables tie money to social position.
vector_explanations.social.reputation.fame_renown: Reputation is a priced trait: +1 to +4 (or negative) reaction modifiers among defined groups, with frequency of recognition setting the point cost.
vector_explanations.social.influence.reaction_rolls: NPCs default to a 3d6 reaction roll modified by appearance, Charisma, and Reputation, read on a table from Disastrous to Excellent unless the GM has fixed their attitude.

title: Savage Worlds Adventure Edition
resolution_core: Trait die + wild d6, both exploding, vs TN 4; each +4 is a raise

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.step_dice: Traits are rated as die types from d4 to d12; raising a skill or attribute steps the die up, so competence is literally the size of the die you throw against TN 4.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.exploding_dice: All trait and damage dice ace: a maximum roll is rerolled and added, letting a lowly d4 occasionally beat a d12 and keeping every roll dangerous.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.bennies: Players start each session with three bennies spent to reroll traits, Soak wounds, recover from Shaken, or draw new action cards, and the GM hands more out for drama and Hindrance play.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.raises: Every 4 points over the target number is a raise: a raise on attack adds +d6 damage, on Support adds +2, and many powers scale effects by raises.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: Rolling 1 on both the trait die and Wild Die is a Critical Failure that cannot be rerolled with a benny, and the GM narrates something going properly wrong.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.static_dc: The default target number is a flat 4 for nearly everything, with situational penalties applied to the roll rather than moving the TN, keeping resolution fast.
vector_explanations.turns.order.card_determined_initiative: Initiative deals poker cards each round — act in descending card order, Jokers act anywhere and grant +2 to all rolls, and Edges like Quick let you redraw low cards.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Wild Cards track up to three Wounds, each imposing a cumulative -1 penalty, with a fourth Wound rolling on the Injury table via Incapacitation; Extras simply drop.
vector_explanations.combat.status.stun_daze: Shaken is the pivotal state: a Shaken character can only move and must roll Spirit to recover, and a second Shaken result from damage becomes a Wound.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: SWADE codifies Distracted (-2 to all trait rolls), Vulnerable (+2 to be hit), Stunned, Entangled, and Bound as formal states that Tests and powers inflict and remove.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.combat_stunts: The Test action lets any skill attack a foe's composure — a taunt with Smarts, a rope-a-dope with Athletics — inflicting Distracted or Vulnerable, or Shaken with a raise.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.multiple_action_pe: A character may take up to three actions in a turn, suffering a -2 penalty on all of them per extra action declared, making action-splitting a deliberate gamble.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Ranged weapons list Short/Medium/Long ranges (e.g., 12/24/48) at 0/-2/-4, with Rate of Fire dice and Recoil layered on for automatic fire.
vector_explanations.combat.scale.mass_combat: The Mass Battles rules abstract armies into tokens with opposed Battle rolls modified by advantages, with player characters rolling to influence the tide each round.
vector_explanations.spatial.geometry.range_measurement: Distances are given in tabletop inches (1 inch = 2 yards) for movement, ranges, and templates — Cone, Blast, and Stream templates physically placed on the table.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Characters spend 5 points to raise the five attributes (Agility, Smarts, Spirit, Strength, Vigor) a die step each from d4, then 12 skill points against those attributes.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Hindrances like Loyal, One Arm, or Wanted are taken at creation for extra build points (Major worth 2, Minor 1), and playing them earns bennies at the table.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Advancement is Edge-driven: every Advance can buy an Edge such as Ambidextrous, Rock and Roll!, or Legendary tier Edges, with Rank gates from Novice to Legendary.
vector_explanations.character.skills.attribute_skill_pairing: Each skill is linked to an attribute; buying a skill above its linked attribute die costs double, nudging builds toward coherent attribute-skill pairings.
vector_explanations.vehicles.chases.chase_ranges: Chases run on abstract Range Increments with action cards dealt each round for positioning; complications on clubs force Maneuvering rolls against the Chase's hazards.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Vehicles have Size, Handling, Top Speed, Toughness with Armor, and Crew ratings, with called shots to the driver and Out of Control tables when maneuvers fail.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.mana_points: Arcane Backgrounds fuel powers with Power Points (typically 10 to start, regenerating 5 per hour), with Shorting and overspending handled by the specific background.
vector_explanations.magic.acquisition.level_up_spell_picks: New powers are gained by taking the New Powers Edge at Advances, chosen from the Arcane Background's list and reskinned freely through Trappings.
vector_explanations.mental.fear.fear_tests: Scary things force Fear checks (Spirit rolls); failure rolls on the Fear Table with results from Distracted through the Mark of Fear to heart-attack-level Terror.
vector_explanations.narrative.structure.interlude_scenes: During travel or downtime the GM can call an Interlude: each player draws a card and tells a story keyed to the suit (Tragedy, Victory, Love, Desire), earning a benny.
vector_explanations.cooperation.assistance.teammate_buff_sup: The Support action rolls a relevant skill to grant an ally +1 (or +2 with a raise) on their next trait roll, stacking up to +4, formalizing teamwork.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.weapon_properties_tags: Weapons carry mechanical tags — AP values that pierce Armor, Min Str requirements, RoF, Reach, Parry bonuses — that interact directly with the Toughness/Armor math.

title: Fate Core
resolution_core: 4dF + skill vs opposition on the adjective ladder; aspects invoked with fate points

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.fate_fudge_dice: Rolls are 4dF (dice faced +, -, blank) added to a skill and read against the adjective ladder, producing a -4 to +4 swing around your rating.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Defend is one of the four actions: attacks and many overcome attempts are met by an opposed 4dF+skill roll, with ties and margins feeding the shift economy.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.fate_points: Fate points are the engine: spend them to invoke aspects, declare story details, or power stunts, and earn them by accepting compels against your own aspects.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.aspect_invocation: Invoking a relevant aspect after a roll grants +2 or a full reroll per fate point (free invokes from create-advantage stack), making aspects the primary modifier system.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.margin_of_success: Outcomes are measured in shifts — the difference between your total and the opposition — with three-plus shifts succeeding with style and earning a boost.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.success_with_cost: Tying on an overcome succeeds at a minor cost, and the GM is explicitly licensed to offer success at a serious cost instead of a flat failure.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Challenges string several overcome rolls against one obstacle, and contests are races to three victories, giving multi-roll structure to non-combat conflict.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.descriptive_ladders: All ratings live on the adjective ladder — Mediocre (+0), Average (+1), Fair (+2), Good (+3) up to Legendary (+8) — spoken as words at the table, not just numbers.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_pyramid: The default skill array is a pyramid: one skill at Great (+4), two at Good (+3), three at Fair (+2), four at Average (+1), enforcing breadth beneath every peak.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.relationshi: The phase trio has each character star in an adventure and then cross into two other characters' stories, generating three aspects that hardwire party history.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Every character has a Trouble aspect — a recurring complication like 'Sucker for a Pretty Face' — that the GM compels for fate points, making the flaw an income source.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.fact_declaration: Players may spend a fate point to declare a story detail — an old contact in this port, a handy fire escape — justified by one of their aspects.
vector_explanations.narrative.authority.success_at_cost_narr: The failure guidance tells GMs to prefer 'succeed at a cost' or failure-with-momentum narration, with the cost calibrated minor or serious to the fiction.
vector_explanations.narrative.worldbuilding.setting_question: Game creation is a group exercise choosing the setting's current and impending issues and casting its faces and places before characters are finalized.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.tracks_aspects: Harm is absorbed by checking stress boxes or taking consequences — aspects like 'Broken Ribs' with one free hostile invoke — rather than depleting hit points.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_track: Characters run parallel physical and mental stress tracks (boxes rated 1-2, extended by Physique and Will), cleared completely at the end of each conflict.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.lingering_injuries: Consequences come in mild (-2), moderate (-4), and severe (-6) slots that absorb that many shifts of harm and persist as invokable aspects until treated and renamed across sessions.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.zones_abstract: Conflicts map space as a handful of named zones; you can attack within your zone (or adjacent with reach), and moving zones is an action or part of one.
vector_explanations.combat.morale.surrender: Conceding before a roll is a formal move: you exit the conflict on your own narrative terms and collect one fate point per consequence taken in the fight.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.milestone_advanceme: Advancement flows from minor milestones (swap/rename), significant milestones (skill point), and major milestones (raise the skill cap, extra refresh) tied to story beats.
vector_explanations.cooperation.assistance.teammate_buff_sup: Allies with a relevant skill at Average or better can combine efforts: one rolls, each helper adds +1, or they stack free invokes via create-advantage teamwork.

title: HERO System 6th Edition
resolution_core: 3d6 roll-under: attacks hit on 11 + OCV - DCV or less; skills on 9 + CHAR/5 or less

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_attr: Attacks succeed on 3d6 rolled at or under 11 + OCV - DCV, and skills at or under 9 + Characteristic/5, so the whole system lives on one roll-under bell curve.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Skill versus Skill contests compare margins of roll-under attempts, and STR contests resolve grabs, escapes, and shoving through opposed Strength rolls.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Every characteristic — STR, DEX, CON, INT, EGO, PRE, plus OCV, DCV, OMCV, DMCV, SPD, PD, ED, REC, END, BODY, STUN — is bought per point from the campaign budget with no random rolls.
vector_explanations.character.progression.build_points: Characters are pure point constructions: a standard superheroic build is 400 points with 75 in matching complications, and powers are priced by Active Points modified by Advantages and Limitations.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Session awards of experience points are spent as ordinary character points, so advancement raises characteristics, buys skills, or adds powers with the same math as creation.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Complications such as Hunted, Psychological Complication, and Vulnerability must total a campaign-set value (75 points at superheroic level) and actively feed the GM plot hooks.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_defaults: Everyman skills give every character an 8- roll in basics like Climbing and Concealment, and Familiarity can be bought for a flat 8- before full 9 + CHAR/5 pricing.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: BODY below zero starts a character dying, losing 1 BODY per turn until stabilized, with death at negative starting BODY — surviving heroes are wounded, not erased.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Damage is dual-tracked: BODY measures lasting physical injury while attacks list separate BODY and STUN totals, with killing attacks threatening BODY directly through resistant defenses.
vector_explanations.combat.status.stun_daze: STUN is its own pool: damage exceeding CON stuns you (losing your next phase), and hitting 0 STUN knocks you out until Recoveries bring you back.
vector_explanations.combat.status.knockback: Superheroic combat rolls Knockback dice against BODY done; excess meters send targets flying into walls for extra damage, with Knockback Resistance bought as a power.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.armor_as_soak: Physical Defense and Energy Defense subtract from incoming STUN and BODY, with Resistant Protection required to stop the BODY of Killing Attacks.
vector_explanations.combat.melee.hit_locations: The optional-but-standard heroic hit location table (3-18) applies STUNx and BODYx multipliers — a head hit at x5 STUN — plus per-location OCV penalties.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.called_shots: Placed shots let attackers pick a hit location at its listed OCV penalty (head -8, vitals -8, hand -6), trading accuracy for wounding multipliers.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.martial_techniques: Martial Arts are bought as maneuver packages — Martial Strike, Martial Throw, Offensive Strike — each with its own OCV/DCV/damage profile, plus Damage Classes to scale them.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: The Grab maneuver seizes a target for STR contests, enabling squeezes, slams, and holding foes as shields, with escape resolved by opposed STR rolls.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.disarm: Disarm is a standard maneuver at -2 OCV resolved as a STR versus STR contest to strip a weapon or focus from an opponent's grip.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.charge: Move Through and Move By add velocity/6 or velocity/10 to damage while costing OCV and risking damage to the attacker, making momentum a codified weapon.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.full_round_actions: Each phase allows a Half Phase move plus a Half Phase action or one Full Phase action; attacking generally ends your phase, so movement-attack sequencing matters.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.held_actions: Characters can hold their phase to act later in the segment order and, crucially, abort a future phase to defensive actions like Dodge or Block when attacked.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Within any segment all acting characters go in descending DEX order, with ties broken by rolls, so bought DEX directly purchases acting first.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Ranged attacks suffer the Range Modifier: -2 OCV per doubling of range beyond 8 meters, halved by weapon scopes or bought off with the No Range Modifier advantage.
vector_explanations.turns.phases.structured_round_phases: Combat runs on the 12-segment Turn with a Post-Segment 12 Recovery for everyone, giving fights a fixed rhythmic skeleton unique to HERO.
vector_explanations.turns.variance.asymmetric_activation_fre: The Speed Chart is the signature mechanic: SPD 2 acts in segments 6 and 12 while SPD 6 acts in 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 — bought SPD literally buys extra turns.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.mana_points: Endurance fuels everything: powers cost 1 END per 10 Active Points each phase, REC restores it at Recoveries, and running dry forces pushing off STUN instead.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.psychic_duels: Mental Powers attack with OMCV against DMCV and are resisted by EGO, with Ego Attack, Mind Control, and Telepathy achieving effect tiers keyed to multiples of the target's EGO.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.gadgeteering: Variable Power Pools let gadgeteers and mages reallocate a floating pool of Active Points between stunts mid-adventure, with control costs and skill rolls governing the swap.
vector_explanations.equipment.durability.charge_depletion: The Charges limitation prices powers by uses per day (e.g., 8 charges at -1/2), modeling clips, potions, and one-shot devices inside the unified power math.
vector_explanations.social.intimidation.displays_of_force: Presence Attacks roll PRE/5 d6 against the target's PRE total, with violent displays adding dice; enough margin makes hardened foes hesitate, obey, or flee.
vector_explanations.stealth.vision.darkvision_senses: Enhanced Senses are itemized powers — Nightvision, Infrared Perception, Danger Sense with its own roll, Targeting for unusual senses — plugged into the PER roll framework.

title: Cyberpunk RED
resolution_core: 1d10 + STAT + skill vs DV or opposed roll; 10s explode once, 1s roll again and subtract

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.exploding_dice: Checks are 1d10 + STAT + skill versus a Difficulty Value or opposed roll; a natural 10 rolls a second d10 and adds it, so any check can spike into a critical success.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: A natural 1 rolls a second d10 and subtracts it, and fumbles in combat trigger the Critical Failure consequences the GM applies to firefights and Netruns alike.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.static_dc: The DV ladder is fixed and published — Everyday (13), Difficult (15), Professional (17), Heroic (21), Incredible (24) — and ranged combat reads DVs straight off weapon range tables.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.lifepath: The Lifepath tables roll cultural origin, personality, clothing style, family background, life goals, friends, enemies, and tragic love affairs, then each Role adds its own Role-specific lifepath.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Ten Roles — Solo, Netrunner, Tech, Medtech, Media, Exec, Lawman, Fixer, Nomad, Rockerboy — each hinge on a unique Role Ability like the Solo's Combat Awareness or the Fixer's Operator.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Advancement spends Improvement Points earned per session on skills and Role Ability ranks, with costs scaling by level, so growth is granular rather than level-based.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is 1d10 + REF rolled at combat start, acting in descending order, with Solos able to add Combat Awareness points to jump the queue.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each turn allows a Move Action (MOVE stat in squares) plus one Action — attack, Netrun a step, stabilize a friend — so turns are fast and brutally simple.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.friday_night_firefight: Combat is the Friday Night Firefight engine: 1d10+REF+weapon skill versus the DV for that weapon type at that range band, deliberately lethal and cover-hungry.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Every weapon type has its own DV row by range band — an SMG is DV15 at 0-6m but DV25 at 51-100m — so weapon choice is literally a table lookup of effectiveness.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.called_shots: Aimed Shots take -8 to hit a specific location: a headshot multiplies damage that penetrates by 2, and leg hits can inflict the Crippled Leg critical injury.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.armor_ablation: Armor Stopping Power subtracts from damage, but every time damage penetrates, that armor location permanently loses 1 SP until repaired — armor is a wasting asset.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Cover has its own hit points (a concrete wall 30 HP, a car door 15), and attackers can literally shoot the cover apart to get at the target behind it.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: HP derive from BODY and WILL; falling below half is Seriously Wounded, imposing a -2 wound state penalty and requiring a Death Save when reduced to 0.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.death_and_dying: At Mortally Wounded you roll a Death Save against BODY each turn, with each failure fatal and each additional wound raising the penalty — Trauma Team response is a purchasable lifeline.
vector_explanations.combat.cyberware.initiative_boost: Speedware like the Kerenzikov adds a flat bonus to initiative rolls while Sandevistan boosts your first turn, at the price of Humanity Loss like all chrome.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cybernetics: Cyberware installs into bodied option slots — cybereyes with up to three options each, cyberarms, borgware — with surgery codes and Eurobuck costs itemized per implant.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cyberwear_humanity: Every implant rolls Humanity Loss dice; EMP (Empathy) drops with Humanity, and hitting rock bottom means cyberpsychosis and losing the character to the GM.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.gadgeteering: The Tech's Maker ability has ranked specialties — Field Expertise, Upgrade Expertise, Fabrication Expertise, Invention Expertise — letting Techs upgrade, build, and invent gear on downtime clocks.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.netrunning: Netrunners physically approach and jack into NET Architectures, descending floor-by-floor structures with Interface Verbs like Scanner, Sword, and Control, acting on the meat and NET in the same round.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.ice_countermeasures: Architectures are guarded by Black ICE like Hellhounds, Wisps, and Kraken with their own stat blocks that attack the runner's brain directly, plus Demons defending control nodes.
vector_explanations.social.reputation.fame_renown: Reputation is a tracked 1-10 score earned through deeds; in a Facedown, both sides roll d10 + Reputation, and losers must back down or act at -2 against you.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.lifestyle_cos: Monthly lifestyle is bought off a table from Kibble (100eb) through Generic Prepack to Luxury, with housing zones priced separately — poverty is mechanically visible.
vector_explanations.downtime.activities.day_job_rolls: Between gigs the Hustle rules let each Role make downtime money — a table of side jobs with rolls determining how many extra Eurobucks the month yields.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.addictions: Street drugs like Boost and Black Lace list Primary and Secondary effects plus an Addiction DV; failing the save hooks the character with withdrawal penalties until they re-dose or detox.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.quality_tiers: Weapons come in Poor, Standard, and Excellent quality — Excellent adds +1 to attack, Poor can jam on a critical failure — and quality is priced into the market tables.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.piloting_checks: Driving and piloting under stress roll REF-based vehicle skills against DVs or opposed checks, with the Nomad's Moto ability granting family vehicles and bonuses behind the wheel.

title: Shadowrun 5th Edition
resolution_core: Attribute + skill d6 dice pool; 5s and 6s are hits vs threshold or opposed hits

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.dice_pool_succe: Tests roll Attribute + Skill (plus modifiers) in d6s, counting 5s and 6s as hits; a street samurai can throw fifteen-plus dice while a civilian throws three.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.success_thresh: Simple tests need 1-4+ hits by difficulty (1 Easy, 4 Very Hard), with net hits above threshold improving results such as damage or information gained.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Extended tests accumulate hits across intervals (minutes to months) with the dice pool shrinking by one die each roll, putting a hard ceiling on grinding.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Attack versus defense, sneaking versus perception, decking versus Firewall — opposed tests compare net hits between two rolled pools, the game's default conflict math.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: A glitch occurs when more than half your dice show 1s; a glitch with zero hits is a critical glitch, licensing the GM to jam guns and crash drones.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.hero_points: Edge is the runner's luck stat: spend it to Push the Limit (add Edge dice with exploding 6s via the Rule of Six), Second Chance a roll, or seize initiative; burn a point permanently to survive.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Priority-grid creation assigns rows A-E across metatype, attributes, skills, magic/resonance, and resources, and every attribute point is then allocated within those budgets.
vector_explanations.character.progression.karma: Advancement spends Karma with published costs — new skill rating x2, attribute rating x5, initiation and submersion grades — so every upgrade is a Karma transaction.
vector_explanations.character.identity.ancestry_heritage: Metatype matters mechanically: trolls get +4 Body range and thermographic vision, elves +1 Agility and low-light, dwarves pathogen resistance, all priced in the priority grid.
vector_explanations.character.identity.flaws_drawbacks: Negative Qualities like Addiction, Bad Luck, or SINner grant bonus Karma at creation, while Positive Qualities like Ambidextrous cost it, capped in each direction.
vector_explanations.character.skills.specialty_trees: Skills belong to groups (Firearms, Electronics) that can be bought whole, and specializations such as Automatics (Assault Rifles) add +2 dice within the niche.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cybernetics: Cyberware and bioware occupy Essence with grades — used, standard, alpha, beta, delta — trading nuyen for lower Essence cost, with capacity slots inside cyberlimbs.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cyberwear_humanity: Every implant burns Essence from the starting 6; lost Essence directly reduces a mage's Magic or technomancer's Resonance, hard-coding the chrome-versus-magic tradeoff.
vector_explanations.simulation.magic.drain: Every spell has a Drain code keyed to Force; after casting, the mage resists Drain with Willpower + tradition attribute, taking stun (or physical, if Force exceeds Magic) damage on failure.
vector_explanations.simulation.magic.summoning_spirits: Conjurers summon spirits of a chosen type at a Force, opposed by the spirit's dice; net hits become owed services, and Drain scales with the spirit's resistance hits.
vector_explanations.simulation.astral.projection: Full magicians can project astrally, leaving their body helpless while their astral form flies at the speed of thought, perceives auras, and fights astral security.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.matrix_host: The Matrix is rebuilt around hosts: deckers place marks (1-3) on targets via Hack on the Fly or Brute Force, with host ratings setting the defending pools for every action inside.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.ice_countermeasures: Hosts launch Intrusion Countermeasures each combat turn — Patrol, Probe, Killer, Track, Black IC — each a distinct program with attack pools that can brick decks or fry brains.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.trace_countdown: Every illegal Matrix action accumulates Overwatch Score; at 40, the Grid Overwatch Division converges, dumping the hacker with dumpshock and dispatching real-world trouble.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.autopilot_drones: Riggers with control rigs jump into vehicles and drones in VR, drones run on Pilot + autosoft dice when autonomous, and the rigger command console juggles multiple machines.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.passes: Initiative is attribute-based plus dice (e.g., REA + INT + 1d6, more dice with Wired Reflexes); after everyone acts, 10 is subtracted from scores and those still positive act again.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Turns are built from Free, Simple, and Complex actions — two Simples or one Complex — with attacks, reloads, and Matrix actions each classified on that ladder.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Damage fills Physical and Stun condition monitors sized by Body and Willpower; every three boxes filled imposes a cumulative -1 wound modifier, and overflow stun becomes physical.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.armor_as_soak: Defenders soak with Body + modified Armor, with weapon AP values eroding armor in the soak pool and attacks whose modified DV exceeds armor becoming physical damage.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.recoil_compensation: Progressive recoil accumulates per bullet fired until the character stops shooting, offset by recoil compensation from gas vents, foregrips, and cyberarms — automatic fire is a resource.
vector_explanations.social.reputation.fame_renown: Runners accrue Street Cred (Karma-based, adds dice to social tests), Notoriety, and Public Awareness, three tracked scores that shape how the shadows receive them.
vector_explanations.social.relationships.loyalty_scores: Contacts are statted with Connection and Loyalty ratings 1-6; those numbers set favors, prices, and networking pools, making the fixer rolodex a mechanical asset.
vector_explanations.equipment.economy_of_gear.availability_r: Buying gear tests against its Availability rating and legality codes (Restricted, Forbidden), with delivery times and black-market markups riding the negotiation.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.lifestyle_cos: Monthly Lifestyles from Street to Luxury cost fixed nuyen and set living conditions, healing rates, and how easily enemies find you.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.addictions: Drugs and BTL chips carry Addiction Ratings and Thresholds with tests after use; failed tests build physiological or psychological addiction levels with withdrawal dice penalties.

title: Eclipse Phase
resolution_core: d100 roll-under skill; doubles crit; margin of success 30+ is excellent

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.roll_under_perc: Tests roll d100 at or under a skill built from aptitude x3 plus points, with situational modifiers in ±10/20/30 steps shifting the target.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.margin_of_success: Margin of success and failure grade outcomes: MoS 30+ is an Excellent success and MoF 30+ a Severe failure, feeding extra damage and complications.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: Rolling doubles (11, 22, 33...) under your target is a critical success — and doubles over it a critical failure — so 00 and 99 bracket the drama.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.hero_points: Moxie is transhuman grit: spend a point to flip-flop a roll's digits (83 becomes 38), upgrade a success, downgrade a critical failure, or ignore trauma penalties for a scene.
vector_explanations.character.lifecycle.resurrection_rules: Death is a logistics problem: cortical stacks and offsite backups restore the ego into a new morph, with rules for backup lag (lost memories) and the insurance costs of restoration.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.incarnation: Characters are egos distinct from their sleeved morphs; sheets track the ego's aptitudes and skills separately from morph bonuses, and resleeving swaps bodies with Integration and Alienation tests.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Creation picks a Background (Infolife AGI, Uplift, Hyperelite, Fall Evacuee) and Faction (Anarchist, Argonaut, Criminal), each granting skill packages and constraints like AGI social stigma.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.point_buy: Chargen distributes customization points across seven aptitudes (COG, COO, INT, REF, SAV, SOM, WIL), skills, traits, and starting gear, with morphs layering bonuses on top.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.derived_attributes: Derived stats do heavy lifting: Lucidity is WIL x2, Trauma Threshold LUC/5, Insanity Rating LUC x2, Durability and Wound Threshold come from the morph — ego and body math interlock.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Rez Points earned in play are spent like creation points to raise skills and aptitudes or buy off negative traits, with costs scaling at skill 60+.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.stress_track: Mental stress accumulates as stress points against Lucidity from horror, ego-violation, and witnessing exsurgent things, the psychological mirror of physical damage.
vector_explanations.mental.stress.trauma_conditions: Stress exceeding the Trauma Threshold in one hit inflicts a trauma, each imposing a cumulative -10 modifier to mental actions and risking disorientation.
vector_explanations.mental.conditions.disorders_treatment: Four accumulated traumas crystallize into a disorder (PTSD, body dysmorphia, fugue states), treatable through weeks of psychotherapy or psychosurgery time rules.
vector_explanations.mental.sanity.sanity_zero_removal: An ego whose stress total reaches its Insanity Rating is irretrievably broken and removed from play — restore from an earlier backup or retire the character.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cybernetics: Morphs mount bioware and cyberware enhancements — medichines, wrist-mounted tools, neurachem — bought as gear and swapped with the body itself when resleeving.
vector_explanations.social.reputation.faction_standing: Reputation is split across networks — @-rep with autonomists, c-rep corporate, i-rep with Firewall, r-rep scientific — each a 0-99 score used where money means nothing.
vector_explanations.social.influence.favor_debts: The favor economy grades asks by level (trivial to acute); calling in favors tests networking against the relevant rep score and burns rep for big requests.
vector_explanations.stealth.hacking.netrunning: Mesh intrusion opposes Infosec against Infosec: hackers gain covert or spotted status, escalate from user to admin privileges, and fight active countermeasures while a trace closes in.
vector_explanations.stealth.concealment.cover_identity: Fake ego IDs are purchasable gear rated by quality, and Firewall sentinels operate under covers whose collapse has mechanical and narrative teeth in a surveillance-saturated habitat network.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.discipline_trees: Asyncs infected by the Watts-MacLeod strain buy Psi trait levels unlocking psi-chi sleights (cognitive boosts) and psi-gamma sleights (telepathy, mind control) as distinct tiers.
vector_explanations.magic.psionics.psychic_strain: Sleights inflict strain as damage to the async's own morph, and pushing sleights amplifies effects at extra strain — psychic power is paid for in nosebleeds.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: Damage against Durability inflicts a wound each time a single hit exceeds the Wound Threshold, each wound stacking -10 to actions until repair or death at Durability x1.5.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.armor_as_soak: Armor has paired Energy and Kinetic ratings subtracted from matching damage types, so a vacsuit shrugs lasers differently than railgun slugs.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Ranged weapons list point-blank, short, medium, long, and extreme ranges with cumulative negative modifiers, and smartlinks offset the ladder.
vector_explanations.turns.variance.asymmetric_activation_fre: The Speed stat (1-4, raised by neurachem and morph tech) grants that many Action Phases per turn, so wired morphs literally act multiple times per round.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.autopilot_drones: Robots and vehicles run on AI pilots, are teleoperated across the mesh, or are directly jammed in VR with the operator's skills substituting for the machine's.
vector_explanations.equipment.crafting.recipe_crafting: Cornucopia machines and desktop fabbers print gear from blueprints and feedstock; blueprints are acquired, pirated, or open source, and nanofab access is a habitat's political currency.
vector_explanations.downtime.recovery.healing_downtime: Biomorphs heal on timescales set by medichines or healing vats (hours to days per wound), while synthmorph damage is repaired with nanobandages and shop time.

title: Lancer
resolution_core: 1d20 + bonuses vs 10 (checks) or Evasion/E-Defense (attacks), with Accuracy/Difficulty d6s

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Skill checks roll 1d20 plus trigger bonuses against a flat 10, while mech attacks roll 1d20 plus Grit or Tech Attack against the target's Evasion or E-Defense.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.boost_setback_dice: Accuracy and Difficulty each add a d6 rolled alongside the d20 — take the highest single d6 and add or subtract it — replacing modifier stacks with dice that cancel one-for-one.
vector_explanations.vehicles.mecha.mech_customization: Mechs are built from a FRAME (Everest to Deathshead) plus weapons in mount sizes and systems bought with System Points, all unlocked through manufacturer license ranks I-III.
vector_explanations.vehicles.mecha.heat_management: Actions like overcharging and tech attacks generate Heat against a Heat Cap; exceeding it forces Engineering checks against reactor stress, with meltdown at the bottom of that spiral.
vector_explanations.vehicles.mecha.pilot_mech_statlines: Pilots have their own HP, triggers, and pilot gear operating at human scale, while the mech tracks HULL/AGI/SYS/ENG-derived stats — pilots can eject, dismount, and die separately.
vector_explanations.combat.damage.structure_damage: Mechs have 4 Structure; each time HP hits zero, Structure drops and the pilot rolls on the structure damage table, risking destroyed weapons, system trauma, or instant destruction.
vector_explanations.combat.scale.mecha_scale: Pilot-scale and mech-scale are distinct layers: pilots caught unmounted against mechs are fragile, pilot weapons barely scratch armor, and Size 1/2 to Size 3+ frames interact with terrain differently.
vector_explanations.character.progression.license_level: Advancement is License Levels 0-12: each LL grants a license rank in a manufacturer's frame line (e.g., IPS-N Blackbeard II), plus scheduled talent, skill, and core bonus picks.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Pilots choose a background like Celebrity, Penal Colonist, or Mercenary that anchors which skill triggers make narrative sense and can be invoked for flavor-appropriate checks.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Narrative play uses skill triggers such as 'Get in Trouble' or 'Take Someone Out' at +2, growing to +4 and +6 with rank investment as LL rises.
vector_explanations.combat.movement.grid_based: Mech combat is played on a grid or hex map with Speed in spaces, Size occupying literal footprint, and rules for engagement, difficult terrain, and forced movement paths.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each turn a mech takes one Full Action or two Quick Actions plus a free protocol and movement, with Overcharge granting an extra Quick Action at escalating Heat cost.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Every mech gets one reaction per turn cycle — Overwatch triggers Skirmish against foes leaving threat, and Brace halves incoming damage at the cost of your next turn's options.
vector_explanations.turns.order.alternating_unit_activation: Initiative alternates: one player activates, then the GM activates an NPC, back and forth, with the side outnumbering the other taking extra consecutive activations at round end.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: A defined condition list — Impaired, Jammed, Lock On, Exposed, Shredded, Slowed, Stunned, Immobilized — carries precise rules text that systems and talents apply and clear.
vector_explanations.combat.status.knockback: Weapons and systems with Knockback X push targets X spaces in a straight line, chaining into hazards, pits, and Impact damage against walls for positional kills.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.grappling: Grapple and Ram are contested Hull checks scaled by Size; grappled mechs are Immobilized or dragged, and ramming knocks targets prone and back a space.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Hard cover grants +2 Difficulty on attacks against you and soft cover +1, with Size determining what blocks line of sight — cover placement is the tactical map's core currency.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.sensor_range: Tech attacks and Lock On only function within your mech's SENSORS range (5-20 by frame), making electronic warfare a positioning game distinct from weapon ranges.
vector_explanations.combat.defense.electronic_warfare: Invade is a tech attack versus E-Defense delivering effects like Fragment Signal (Impaired and Slowed), while systems like ECM fields and the Goblin's puppetry weaponize the datasphere.
vector_explanations.logistics.maintenance.repairs: Each mech carries a Repair Cap; during a Rest the pilot spends repairs to restore HP, clear reactor stress, and patch structure, while a Full Repair between missions resets everything.
vector_explanations.downtime.structure.downtime_scenes: Between missions pilots take structured downtime actions — Get Organized, Get Connected, Gather Information, Get Focused — each with codified outcomes on a success ladder.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.weapon_properties_tags: Weapons carry tags like Accurate, Loading, Ordnance, Smart, Seeking, and Armor-Piercing whose rules interlock with talents (e.g., Walking Armory) and mount restrictions.
vector_explanations.equipment.durability.charge_depletion: Limited X systems carry only X charges per Full Repair, and core power is a once-per-mission resource, so the biggest guns and CORE abilities are strictly rationed.

title: Starfinder
resolution_core: d20 + modifiers vs DC, or vs EAC/KAC for attacks

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Everything rides the d20: skill checks and saves against DCs, attacks against Energy or Kinetic Armor Class, with class-derived Base Attack Bonus and ability modifiers.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Seven core classes — Envoy, Mechanic, Mystic, Operative, Solarian, Soldier, Technomancer — deliver scripted features per level, like the Solarian's stellar mode attunement cycle.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Every character takes a Theme (Ace Pilot, Bounty Hunter, Scholar) granting an ability score boost, a theme knowledge skill benefit, and features at levels 6, 12, and 18.
vector_explanations.character.identity.ancestry_heritage: Playable species like androids, kasathas, shirrens, vesk, and ysoki carry ability adjustments, size, and racial traits (android flat affect, ysoki cheek pouches) with mechanical weight.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: Advancement follows a fixed XP table to level 20, with encounter Challenge Ratings translating into XP budgets for the GM's encounter math.
vector_explanations.character.progression.saving_throws: Fortitude, Reflex, and Will saves scale on good/poor class progressions, defending against poisons, blast templates, and mind-affecting magic respectively.
vector_explanations.character.skills.skill_ranks: Characters spend skill ranks (capped at character level) each level, with class skills granting a +3 bonus once trained, on a consolidated Pathfinder-derived skill list.
vector_explanations.character.skills.class_skill_lists: Each class defines its class skills and skill ranks per level — Operatives get 8 + INT and Skill Focus expertise, Soldiers 4 + INT — making skill breadth a class identity.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.hit_points: Damage depletes Stamina Points first and Hit Points second; SP return with a 10-minute rest spending a Resolve Point, while HP recover slowly or via magic and medicine.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.death_defiance_points: Resolve Points (from level and key ability) stabilize you when dying, let you stay in the fight at 0 HP, and power class features like the Envoy's improvisations.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.full_round_actions: Turns follow the standard/move/swift/full-action economy: full attacks trade movement for two attacks at -4, and spells and reloads are slotted by action type.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.reactions_opportun: Leaving a threatened square or casting adjacent to a foe provokes attacks of opportunity, one reaction per round, shaping tactical movement around melee threat.
vector_explanations.combat.initiative.dexterity_based: Initiative is d20 + Dexterity modifier (plus feats like Improved Initiative), rolled once and held for the encounter in descending order.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.square_grid: Combat assumes a 5-foot-square grid with speed in feet, diagonal movement counting 5-10-5, reach, and area templates mapped to squares.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.flanking: Melee attackers on opposite sides of a target gain +2 flanking bonuses, so battlefield geometry rewards coordinated positioning.
vector_explanations.combat.positioning.cover_system: Cover grants +4 AC and +2 Reflex (improved and partial variants scale it), and soft cover from creatures blocks attacks of opportunity math.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: A glossary of conditions — asleep, bleeding, burning, dazed, encumbered, entangled, flat-footed, off-kilter, off-target — carries exact rules referenced by weapons and spells.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.armor_types: Armor is light or heavy with paired EAC and KAC bonuses, maximum Dex caps, and upgrade slots; powered armor adds its own Strength and reach profile.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.weapon_properties_tags: Weapons have categories (laser, plasma, projectile, cryo) and special properties like boost, automatic, unwieldy, and line, plus critical hit effects such as burn or corrode.
vector_explanations.equipment.durability.charge_depletion: Energy weapons drain battery charges per shot and can be recharged at stations; projectile weapons track rounds, making usage-per-fight a logistics layer.
vector_explanations.equipment.crafting.recipe_crafting: Crafting converts Universal Polymer Base (UPBs) into gear of item levels you can access with the right skill ranks, standardizing fabrication across the setting.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_by_lev: Item levels pace the economy: gear is rated 1-20, character wealth-by-level tables steer purchases, and selling prices are fixed at 10% of list.
vector_explanations.magic.casting.spell_slots: Mystics and Technomancers cast from spells known using per-day spell slots of levels 0-6, with 0-level spells at will and slot counts scaling on class tables.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Starship combat assigns each PC a role — Captain, Pilot, Gunner, Engineer, Science Officer — with role-specific actions (Encourage, Evasive Maneuvers, Divert, Scan) each round.
vector_explanations.vehicles.combat.shield_facing: Ship shields are allocated across forward, port, starboard, and aft quadrants; the Science Officer's Balance action redistributes Shield Points between arcs mid-fight.
vector_explanations.vehicles.combat.firing_arcs: Ship weapons mount in forward, port, starboard, aft, or turret arcs, so the Pilot's stunts and facing determine which guns can bear each gunnery phase.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Starships are statted with tiers, Hull Points, Armor Class, Target Lock, Damage Threshold, and Critical Thresholds, with damage degrading specific systems like engines and weapons arrays.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.ship_customization: Ships are built from Build Points by tier: frames from racer to dreadnought, power cores, thrusters, computers, expansion bays, and weapons — the party ship grows with their tier.
vector_explanations.vehicles.travel.space_jump_travel: Interstellar travel goes through the Drift: engine ratings 1-2+ divide 1d6-day transits to Absalom Station or 5d6 days to Far Space, with Drift beacons anchoring navigation.

title: Numenera
resolution_core: d20 vs difficulty x 3; skills, assets, and Effort lower the difficulty; GM never rolls

vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.d20_vs_dc: Players roll d20 against a target of the task's difficulty times three; rolls of 17-20 add damage or effects, and all modifications move the difficulty rather than the die.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.tiered_difficu: Every task is rated difficulty 0-10 (target 0-30); skills, assets, and Effort each ease the task by steps of one difficulty level, and difficulty 0 tasks succeed automatically without a roll.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.effort_spend: Applying a level of Effort costs 3 points from the relevant Pool (plus 2 per additional level), reduced by Edge — converting your Might, Speed, or Intellect into eased tasks or extra damage.
vector_explanations.resolution.modifiers.situational_modifie: Assets — the right tool, a helpful circumstance, an ally's distraction — each ease a task by one step, capped at two assets per task, sitting alongside skill and Effort.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.gm_intrusions: The GM intrudes on the story ('your glaive jams in the door'), offering 2 XP — one of which must be given to another player — or the player pays 1 XP to refuse.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_success: A natural 19 grants a minor effect and a natural 20 a major effect (or free Effort), with combat 17-18 adding +1/+2 damage — high rolls buy narrative extras.
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.critical_failure: A natural 1 always triggers a free GM intrusion — an immediate complication with no XP compensation — building risk into every roll the players make.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.reroll_resources: Any roll can be rerolled by spending 1 XP, taking the better result, which makes XP a live currency at the table rather than pure savings.
vector_explanations.meta.player_facing.gm_never_rolls: The GM never rolls dice: monsters' attacks are defense rolls by players, and creature levels set target numbers, moving all randomness to the player side of the screen.
vector_explanations.character.attributes.pool_based_attribut: Characters have no ability scores, only Might, Speed, and Intellect Pools with an Edge rating in each; Edge discounts costs and Pools are both fuel and life.
vector_explanations.logistics.survival.stat_pools_effort: The same Pools that power Effort absorb damage — Might damage from swords, Intellect from mental assault — so spending aggressively leaves you literally closer to death.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.class_based: Characters are a sentence: 'I am an [adjective] [noun] who [verbs]' — the noun being the type (Glaive, Nano, Jack) that sets Pools, Edge, and tier abilities like Fighting Moves and Esoteries.
vector_explanations.character.character_creation.tags_traits: The descriptor (Clever, Graceful, Mystical) and focus (Bears a Halo of Fire, Controls Beasts) bolt traits, skills, and abilities onto the type, completing the character sentence.
vector_explanations.character.progression.experience_points: XP from discoveries and intrusions buys benefits in sets of four (Pool points, Edge, Effort, skills) to climb tiers 1-6 — advancement flows from exploration, not kills.
vector_explanations.character.skills.freeform_skills: Skills are open-ended descriptors; being trained eases related tasks one step and specialized two steps, with no master skill list constraining what can be learned.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.wound_levels: The damage track runs Hale, Impaired, Debilitated, Dead as Pools empty: Impaired raises Effort costs, Debilitated stops all action but crawling — a three-stage collapse.
vector_explanations.combat.ranged.range_brackets: Distance uses abstract bands — Immediate, Short, Long (and Very Long) — with movement and weapon ranges phrased in those bands rather than feet or squares.
vector_explanations.downtime.recovery.recovery_tests: Recovery rolls (1d6 + tier) restore Pool points on a fixed ladder: one action, ten minutes, one hour, ten hours — four rests of increasing length per day.
vector_explanations.simulation.technology.cyphers_limit: Cyphers — one-use godtech like detonations and stim doses — drop constantly but each character can only safely carry a limit (typically 2-3), forcing constant use and churn.
vector_explanations.equipment.gear.artifact_rules: Artifacts are persistent numenera with levels and a depletion roll (e.g., 1 in 1d20) checked each use, so every activation of a found wonder gambles its future.
