vector_explanations.equipment.gear.item_identification: Understanding a found cypher or artifact is itself a task — an Intellect check informed by understanding numenera skill and time spent — before the device can be safely used.
vector_explanations.cooperation.assistance.teammate_buff_sup: Helping is codified: an assisting character trained in the task eases it a step (or provides an asset), and multiple helpers on physical tasks add Effort levels.

title: The Expanse Roleplaying Game
resolution_core: 3d6 (one is the Drama Die) + ability + focus vs TN; doubles generate stunt points

vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.tiered_difficu: Tests roll 3d6 + ability (+2 for an applicable focus) against target numbers on a ladder from Routine (7) and Average (9) through Challenging (11) to Nigh Impossible (21).
vector_explanations.resolution.degrees.degrees_of_success: One die is a different color — the Drama Die — and its face value measures how well you succeeded, from bare success at 1 to spectacular results at 6, and sets stunt points.
vector_explanations.resolution.core_mechanic.opposed_rolls: Opposed tests pit both sides' 3d6 + ability + focus totals against each other, with the Drama Die breaking ties — grapples, deceptions, and dogfights all resolve this way.
vector_explanations.resolution.target_numbers.extended_tests: Advanced tests accumulate Drama Die results across successful rolls toward a success threshold (5, 10, 15, 25), turning long tasks like repairs and research into paced sequences.
vector_explanations.combat.maneuvers.combat_stunts: Rolling doubles on any successful test generates stunt points equal to the Drama Die, spent immediately from stunt menus — Knock Prone, Pierce Armor, Bon Mot — across combat, social, and exploration scenes.
vector_explanations.resolution.luck.luck_burn: Fortune is the buffer between characters and harm: spend Fortune to reduce incoming damage or improve a test result, and characters risk real injury only once Fortune runs thin.
vector_explanations.resolution.metacurrency.threat_pool: The Churn is a GM pool that fills as the story simmers; at thresholds the GM spends it on complications, twists, and escalations, mechanizing the books' sense of everything going sideways.
vector_explanations.character.identity.backgrounds: Characters combine an origin — Earther, Martian, or Belter, each with mechanical traits and gravity acclimatization — with a background like Outsider or Corporate that grants ability focuses and talents.
vector_explanations.character.progression.professions: A profession (Pilot, Fixer, Scavenger, Soldier) caps character creation, granting a starting focus and talent, and frames what work the character knows.
vector_explanations.character.identity.values_drives: Each character has a Drive — Achiever, Caregiver, Survivor — that grants talents and improvements plus a defining quality and downfall, tying motivation to mechanics.
vector_explanations.character.progression.perks: Talents like Fringer, Hacking, or Overwhelm Attack come in Novice, Expert, and Master degrees that deepen with level, alongside specializations at higher levels.
vector_explanations.character.skills.attribute_skill_pairing: Nine abilities (Accuracy, Communication, Dexterity, Fighting, Intelligence, Perception, Strength, Toughness, Willpower) carry focuses like Dexterity (Free-Fall) or Communication (Deception) worth +2 within the niche.
vector_explanations.campaign.progression.milestone_advanceme: Characters advance in levels 1-20 awarded at story milestones — typically an adventure's end — rather than tallied XP, with each level granting scheduled improvements.
vector_explanations.combat.status.conditions_tracking: Running out of Fortune brings conditions — Injured and Wounded impose test penalties, Dying starts the clock — plus situational states like Fatigued, Prone, and Restrained with defined effects.
vector_explanations.combat.wounds.armor_as_soak: Armor has a rating subtracted from incoming damage (with Penetrating attacks ignoring it), and heavier armor trades Speed for soak in the gear tables.
vector_explanations.turns.order.rolled_initiative: Combat opens with 3d6 + Dexterity (or Perception with the right focus) initiative tests, acting in descending order through 15-second rounds.
vector_explanations.combat.action_economy.minor_major_action: Each turn allows a major action and a minor action (or two minors): attacks and full defense are major, aiming, moving, and readying are minor — clean AGE-system economy.
vector_explanations.economy.wealth_abstraction.wealth_levels: Money is abstracted into an Income score; purchases test Income against the item's cost rating, with success sometimes depleting Income — no ledger of credits.
vector_explanations.narrative.structure.interlude_scenes: Between adventures, interludes give each character a downtime scene — recovering, working relationships, pursuing projects or income — with light mechanical outcomes.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.crew_positions: Spaceship combat assigns crew roles — pilot maneuvering, gunner firing torpedoes and PDCs, engineer patching systems — each contributing tests to the ship's round.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.vehicle_stats_blocks: Ships are statted with Size, Hull, Sensors, Juice (thrust reserves), weapons, and Competence, a full profile independent of the crew's own sheets.
vector_explanations.vehicles.combat.component_damage: Ship damage inflicts Losses — hits are absorbed by choosing losses to systems, crew, or the hull itself — so a running battle degrades specific capabilities the crew must triage.
vector_explanations.vehicles.operation.piloting_checks: Flying under thrust is tested piloting: hard burns, flip-and-burn maneuvers, and evasive action call for Dexterity (Piloting) tests, with high-g maneuvers punishing the crew.
